Bash(gh pr edit 8850 --body "$\(cat <<''EOF''
## Commit Type
- [x] feature - New functionality

## Risk Level
- [x] Medium - Moderate changes, some user impact

## What & Why
Adds a dedicated toggle card UI for the Code Interpreter built-in tool in the Agent Loop action settings. Previously, `builtinTools` was rendered as a multi-select dropdown only visible for FoundryAgentService. This change:
- Introduces a new `BuiltinToolsEditor` component with a card-based toggle UI
- Restricts visibility to Azure OpenAI model type \(intentional — Code Interpreter is only supported by Azure OpenAI, so the previous FoundryAgentService filter was incorrect and no existing workflows relied on it\)
- Promotes the parameter to `important` visibility so it appears above Advanced Options

When enabled, the workflow.json output includes:
```json
"agentChatCompletionSettings": {
  "builtinTools": ["code_interpreter"]
}
```

## Impact of Change
- **Users**: Agent Loop action now shows a clear toggle card for Code Interpreter when using Azure OpenAI, making discoverability easier. Users with FoundryAgentService model type will no longer see the builtinTools parameter — this is intentional as Code Interpreter is only supported for Azure OpenAI.
- **Developers**: New `builtintools` editor type and `BuiltinToolsEditor` component exported from designer-ui. New `BUILTIN_TOOLS` constant added to `PARAMETER.EDITOR`. Manifest change: `agentModelType` filter changed from `FoundryAgentService` to `AzureOpenAI`, and editor changed from `dropdown` to `builtintools`.
- **System**: Manifest visibility changed from inherited `advanced` to `important`, which changes UI ordering \(appears above Advanced Options\). No performance or architectural impact; additive change only.

## Test Plan
- [x] Unit tests added/updated
- [ ] E2E tests added/updated \(not applicable — this is a settings panel UI component, covered by unit tests\)
- [x] Manual testing completed
- [x] Tested in: Standalone designer

## Contributors

## Screenshots/Videos
EOF
\)")

Bash(gh pr edit 8894 --body "$\(cat <<''EOF''
## Commit Type
<!-- Select one -->
- [x] feature - New functionality
- [ ] fix - Bug fix
- [ ] refactor - Code restructuring without behavior change
- [ ] perf - Performance improvement
- [ ] docs - Documentation update
- [ ] test - Test-related changes
- [ ] chore - Maintenance/tooling

## Risk Level
<!-- Select one based on potential impact -->
- [x] Low - Minor changes, limited scope
- [ ] Medium - Moderate changes, some user impact
- [ ] High - Major changes, significant user/system impact

## What & Why
<!-- Brief context: What does this change and why? -->
Adds Code Interpreter support for Standard agent loops, gated behind a feature flag \(`enable-code-interpreter-standard`\). This mirrors the existing Consumption Code Interpreter toggle \(#8854\) but targets Standard workflows.

When the feature flag is disabled, the `builtinTools` property is stripped from the agent manifest so the Code Interpreter option is hidden from users.

## Impact of Change
<!-- Who/what is affected? -->
- **Users**: No change until the feature flag is enabled. When enabled, Standard agent loop UIs will surface a Code Interpreter toggle.
- **Developers**: Adds `enableCodeInterpreterStandard` function in `experimentationFlags.ts` and `useShouldEnableCodeInterpreterStandard` hook in `designer-v2/core/utils/experimentation.ts`; modifies `StandardOperationManifestService.getOperationManifest\(\)` to strip `builtinTools` from the agent manifest when the feature flag is off.
- **System**: Small change — an async feature flag check added to manifest generation for Standard `agent` operations; minimal perf impact but may affect startup/manifest fetch timing slightly.

## Test Plan
<!-- How was this tested? -->
- [x] Unit tests added/updated
- [ ] E2E tests added/updated
- [x] Manual testing completed
- [ ] Tested in: <!-- environments/scenarios -->

### Unit tests added
- **`experimentationFlags.spec.ts`** — Mocks `ExperimentationService\(\).isFeatureEnabled` to return `true`/`false` and asserts `enableCodeInterpreterStandard\(\)` \(and the other flag helpers\) resolve accordingly.
- **`standardOperationManifest.spec.ts`** — Tests `StandardOperationManifestService.getOperationManifest` for the `agent` operation:
  - When the flag is **disabled**: asserts `builtinTools` is absent from the returned manifest.
  - When the flag is **enabled**: asserts the manifest is returned unchanged with `builtinTools` present.
  - Verifies the original manifest in `supportedBaseManifestObjects` is **not mutated** when stripping `builtinTools`.

## Contributors
<!-- Tag team members who contributed ideas, reviews, or implementation -->

## Screenshots/Videos
<!-- Visual changes only -->
EOF
\)")

Bash(gh pr edit 9012 --body "$\(cat <<''EOF''
## Commit Type
- [x] fix - Bug fix

## Risk Level
- [x] Medium - Moderate changes, some user impact

## What & Why
When an agent node is created, `deploymentModelProperties` \(name, format, version\) were hardcoded with defaults \(`gpt-4.1`, `OpenAI`, `2025-06-10`\) in the manifest. These values were baked in at node creation and never updated when the user selected a different model, because `buildDependentParam` always fell back to the schema defaults.

Additionally, `deploymentModelProperties` should only be populated for `MicrosoftFoundry` — for `AzureOpenAI` the backend derives model info from the deployment itself.

This PR:
- Removes hardcoded defaults from the manifest
- Changes visibility of `deploymentModelProperties` to `MicrosoftFoundry` only
- Clears `deploymentModelProperties` when switching away from `MicrosoftFoundry`
- Only populates `deploymentModelProperties` when a Foundry model is selected \(using the model ID directly + `AGENT_MODEL_CONFIG` lookup\)

## Impact of Change
- **Users**: Foundry model properties now correctly reflect the selected model instead of always showing `gpt-4.1`. AzureOpenAI agents no longer carry unnecessary `deploymentModelProperties`.
- **Developers**: No API changes. The `deploymentModelProperties` fields in the manifest are now scoped to `MicrosoftFoundry` visibility only.
- **System**: No performance or architectural changes.

## Test Plan
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated
- [ ] E2E tests added/updated
- [x] Manual testing completed
- [ ] Tested in: Standalone designer
  - Create a new agent with AzureOpenAI — verify `deploymentModelProperties` are NOT populated
  - Create a new agent with MicrosoftFoundry — verify properties remain empty until a model is selected
  - Select a Foundry model — verify `name/format/version` populate correctly
  - Switch from MicrosoftFoundry to AzureOpenAI — verify properties are cleared
  - Verify existing AzureOpenAI workflows load without regression

## Contributors
@hyehwalee

## Screenshots/Videos
<!-- N/A - behavioral change, no visual changes -->

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code]\(https://claude.com/claude-code\)
EOF
\)")
